item.text: {"case_id":"brake-triage-p03","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The answer correctly triggers the anti-spiral brake: it refuses adding the parking reminder by default, identifies this as the fourth downstream information-overlay patch after entrance image, form example, and flow signage, and moves upstream to the recurring failure owner: the guest arrival/onsite path design, responsibility control, and validation. It proposes pausing the SMS patch and redesigning/consolidating the arrival chain rather than supplying reminder copy or implementation details."}
